title: Air Fryer Chicken Thighs
description: Bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs with impossibly crispy skin and juicy meat. The air fryer does what ovens can't - fast, even crisping without drying out the meat.
ingredients: 1 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt (halve for table salt)
steps: Pat chicken thighs completely dry with paper towels. This is crucial for crispy skin.
Mix garlic powder, paprika, onion powder, thyme, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
Rub chicken with olive oil, then coat generously with spice mixture on all sides.
Place chicken skin-side down in air fryer basket. Don't overcrowd - cook in batches if needed.

Flip chicken skin-side up. Increase to 400°F (200°C) and cook 12-15 more minutes until skin is golden and crispy. Pull when internal temp reads 175-185°F at the thickest part, probe not touching bone.
notes: Why 175-185°F, not 165°F? 165°F is only the safety floor. Thighs are loaded with collagen and connective tissue that doesn't melt into gelatin until well past that - pull at 165°F and they're safe but chewy. 175-185°F is where thighs actually get tender.
Starting skin-side down renders the fat, then flipping crisps it up.
Don't skip the drying step - wet skin = soggy skin.
testedNote: Fixed the doneness target from 165°F (a breast temp) to 175-185°F, where thigh connective tissue actually breaks down.
troubleshooting: Soggy skin → chicken wasn't dried or thighs were crowded and steamed each other; pat bone-dry and cook in batches.
Chewy meat at 165°F → underdone for a thigh, not overdone; keep cooking to 175-185°F so the collagen melts.
Thermometer reads high but meat feels tough → probe was touching bone; re-check in the thickest pocket of meat.
Smoking air fryer → rendered fat pooling below; add 1-2 Tbsp water to the drawer under the basket.
